### Post by jamespickles on 2008-02-14
Well i just followed "forlongs" guide on how to setp up the new fglrx ati driver with aiglx support, i followed the guide to the end, everything seems fine, i then went to open catalyst control center it said "no ati driver is installed or it isnt functioning properly"

### Post by tangibleorange on 2008-02-14
You could try adding:

> Load     "fglrx" 

To the "Module" section...

Make sure you restart X afterwards! (Ctrl-Alt-Backspace)

---

### Post by danwood76 on 2008-02-14
There are a couple of sections in your xorg.conf that dont need to be there.
Like:
```

Section "Module"
	Load		"glx"
	Load		"dbe"
	Load		"dri"
	Load		"v4l"
EndSection
Section "device" # 
	Identifier	"device1"
	Boardname	"ATI Radeon (fglrx)"
	Busid		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	Screen	1
	Vendorname	"ATI"
EndSection
Section "screen" # 
	Identifier	"screen1"
	Device		"device1"
	Defaultdepth	24
	Monitor		"monitor1"
EndSection
Section "monitor" # 
	Identifier	"monitor1"
	Gamma	1.0
EndSection
Section "ServerFlags"
EndSection

```

But that wont be affecting it I doubt.

When you installed the latest ati driver did you build it with this command:
```

./ati-driver-installer-8-02-x86.x86_64.run --buildpkg Ubuntu/gutsy

```

Then install the *.debs after?
As that is the way to do it, I did it this morning with no errors.

You could try the ati config script to automatically setup your xorg.conf:
```

sudo aticonfig --initial

```

If you have the ati modules installed correctly that last command will setup the xserver correctly.

---
